title: Slow Simmered Moroccan Tomatoes Tangia
description: A funky and cozy Moroccan dish featuring chopped tomatoes, this Whole30-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner. It is made with a whisk and cooked in a tangia.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tangia over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped tomatoes and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the tangia hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Traditionally, the tangia is cooked in a clay pot with a narrow neck, which allows the steam to condense and drop back into the dish, creating a rich and flavorful sauce.
